title: tragacantha oblongifolia
intro: Tragacantha oblongifolia, also known as the oblong-leaved milk thistle or oblong-leaved thistle, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to protect the liver and promote digestion. The seeds of Tragacantha oblongifolia contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, leading to potential health benefits such as improved liver function, reduced oxidative stress, and enhanced immune system function. Herbal preparations of Tragacantha oblongifolia, including capsules, tablets, and teas, are often used to support liver health and promote overall well-being.
